rules.title: Stage >=3 pressure injury with purulent exudate suggests infection risk

then.rationale: Stage >=3 pressure injury with purulent exudate suggests infection risk. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Obtain a wound culture and evaluate for infection.
then.clinical_impact: Untreated wound infection risks tissue loss, sepsis, and delayed healing.

rules.title: Chronic wound with no size reduction after two weeks of standard care needs clinician reassessment

then.rationale: Chronic wound with no size reduction after two weeks of standard care needs clinician reassessment. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Reassess the wound and evaluate for chronic wound infection or advanced therapy.
then.clinical_impact: A chronic wound failing to reduce despite standard care may harbor infection or biofilm; delayed reassessment lets it deteriorate.

rules.title: Purulent exudate with periwound erythema and warmth suggests local infection

then.rationale: Purulent exudate with periwound erythema and warmth suggests local infection. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Escalate to a wound specialist for suspected local infection.
then.clinical_impact: Local wound infection can progress to cellulitis or systemic infection if unaddressed.

rules.title: Foul odor with heavy exudate and increasing pain suggests infection or necrotic burden

then.rationale: Foul odor with heavy exudate and increasing pain suggests infection or necrotic burden. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Evaluate for debridement of necrotic or infected tissue.
then.clinical_impact: Necrotic burden and infection impede healing and can seed systemic infection.

rules.title: Surgical wound with purulent drainage and dehisced edge suggests surgical site infection

then.rationale: Surgical wound with purulent drainage and dehisced edge suggests surgical site infection. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Notify the provider immediately for suspected surgical site infection.
then.clinical_impact: Surgical site infection with dehiscence can progress rapidly to deep infection.

rules.title: Stage 1 pressure injury with nonblanchable erythema needs offloading

then.rationale: Stage 1 pressure injury with nonblanchable erythema needs offloading. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Initiate offloading and a repositioning schedule.
then.clinical_impact: Early pressure damage can progress to full-thickness injury without offloading.

rules.title: Stage 2 pressure injury with serous exudate needs moisture-balancing dressing

then.rationale: Stage 2 pressure injury with serous exudate needs moisture-balancing dressing. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Apply a moisture-balancing dressing for partial-thickness tissue loss.
then.clinical_impact: Partial-thickness loss heals best with balanced moisture; imbalance stalls healing.

rules.title: Stage 3 pressure injury with slough needs wound specialist review

then.rationale: Stage 3 pressure injury with slough needs wound specialist review. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Refer for wound specialist review of the advanced pressure injury.
then.clinical_impact: Advanced full-thickness injury with slough needs specialist-directed management.

rules.title: Stage 4 pressure injury with exposed bone, tendon, or muscle needs urgent multidisciplinary care

then.rationale: Stage 4 pressure injury with exposed bone, tendon, or muscle needs urgent multidisciplinary care. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Arrange urgent multidisciplinary wound care.
then.clinical_impact: Exposed deep structures risk osteomyelitis and require coordinated care.

rules.title: Sacral or heel pressure injury with limited mobility has high recurrence risk

then.rationale: Sacral or heel pressure injury with limited mobility has high recurrence risk. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Provide a pressure-redistribution support surface.
then.clinical_impact: High-risk sites with limited mobility recur without redistribution surfaces.

rules.title: Diabetic foot ulcer with reduced sensation on the plantar surface fits a neuropathic pattern

then.rationale: Diabetic foot ulcer with reduced sensation on the plantar surface fits a neuropathic pattern. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Evaluate for an offloading device.
then.clinical_impact: Neuropathic plantar ulcers worsen without pressure offloading.

rules.title: Diabetic foot ulcer with a positive probe-to-bone test suggests osteomyelitis risk

then.rationale: Diabetic foot ulcer with a positive probe-to-bone test suggests osteomyelitis risk. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Order imaging and refer to a specialist for osteomyelitis workup.
then.clinical_impact: Probe-to-bone positivity substantially raises osteomyelitis likelihood.

rules.title: Diabetic foot ulcer with purulent exudate and periwound erythema suggests diabetic foot infection

then.rationale: Diabetic foot ulcer with purulent exudate and periwound erythema suggests diabetic foot infection. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Arrange same-day clinical evaluation for diabetic foot infection.
then.clinical_impact: Diabetic foot infection can progress rapidly to limb-threatening infection.

rules.title: Diabetic foot ulcer with callus and hyperkeratotic edge indicates excess pressure or shear

then.rationale: Diabetic foot ulcer with callus and hyperkeratotic edge indicates excess pressure or shear. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Provide offloading and callus management.
then.clinical_impact: Uncontrolled pressure and shear perpetuate ulceration.

rules.title: Diabetic foot ulcer with poor glucose control and delayed healing risks impaired healing

then.rationale: Diabetic foot ulcer with poor glucose control and delayed healing risks impaired healing. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Review diabetes management to support wound healing.
then.clinical_impact: Hyperglycemia impairs healing and increases infection risk.

rules.title: Venous leg ulcer with edema in the medial gaiter area fits a venous insufficiency pattern

then.rationale: Venous leg ulcer with edema in the medial gaiter area fits a venous insufficiency pattern. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Assess compression eligibility.
then.clinical_impact: Untreated venous insufficiency perpetuates ulceration.

rules.title: Venous leg ulcer with heavy exudate and maceration risks periwound moisture damage

then.rationale: Venous leg ulcer with heavy exudate and maceration risks periwound moisture damage. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Increase dressing absorbency and protect the periwound skin.
then.clinical_impact: Maceration enlarges the wound and delays healing.

rules.title: Arterial ulcer on toes or foot with rest pain and diminished pulses suggests ischemia

then.rationale: Arterial ulcer on toes or foot with rest pain and diminished pulses suggests ischemia. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Refer for vascular assessment of suspected ischemia.
then.clinical_impact: Ischemia risks tissue necrosis and limb loss without revascularization.

rules.title: Arterial ulcer with pale or dry bed and delayed capillary refill indicates poor perfusion

then.rationale: Arterial ulcer with pale or dry bed and delayed capillary refill indicates poor perfusion. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Avoid compression until vascular status is confirmed.
then.clinical_impact: Compression on a poorly perfused limb can cause tissue damage.

rules.title: Mixed etiology leg ulcer with both venous and arterial signs needs ABI/TBI before compression

then.rationale: Mixed etiology leg ulcer with both venous and arterial signs needs ABI/TBI before compression. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Obtain ABI/TBI before applying compression.
then.clinical_impact: Mixed disease requires perfusion measurement to compress safely.

rules.title: Necrotic eschar with infection signs suggests infected necrotic tissue

then.rationale: Necrotic eschar with infection signs suggests infected necrotic tissue. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Evaluate urgently for debridement of infected necrotic tissue.
then.clinical_impact: Infected eschar can seed deep and systemic infection.

rules.title: Slough over half the wound bed with moderate to heavy exudate is a high devitalized-tissue burden

then.rationale: Slough over half the wound bed with moderate to heavy exudate is a high devitalized-tissue burden. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Review the debridement method for a high devitalized-tissue burden.
then.clinical_impact: Devitalized tissue harbors bacteria and blocks granulation.

rules.title: Granulating wound bed with light to moderate exudate and no infection has a favorable trajectory

then.rationale: Granulating wound bed with light to moderate exudate and no infection has a favorable trajectory. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Continue moist wound healing to protect the favorable trajectory.
then.clinical_impact: Disrupting a healing bed can reverse progress.

rules.title: Epithelializing wound with decreasing size shows healing progress

then.rationale: Epithelializing wound with decreasing size shows healing progress. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Protect the new epithelial tissue.
then.clinical_impact: Fragile new epithelium is easily disrupted.

rules.title: Heavy exudate with maceration risks moisture-associated skin damage

then.rationale: Heavy exudate with maceration risks moisture-associated skin damage. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Apply a barrier film and a more absorbent dressing.
then.clinical_impact: Moisture-associated damage enlarges the wound and delays healing.

rules.title: Periwound erythema with adhesive injury indicates medical adhesive-related skin injury

then.rationale: Periwound erythema with adhesive injury indicates medical adhesive-related skin injury. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Switch to skin-friendly securement.
then.clinical_impact: Repeated adhesive trauma degrades periwound skin integrity.

rules.title: Indurated periwound with increasing pain and warmth raises concern for cellulitis

then.rationale: Indurated periwound with increasing pain and warmth raises concern for cellulitis. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Arrange provider evaluation for suspected cellulitis.
then.clinical_impact: Cellulitis can progress to systemic infection if untreated.

rules.title: Dry, cracked periwound with none or scant exudate risks periwound breakdown

then.rationale: Dry, cracked periwound with none or scant exudate risks periwound breakdown. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Apply hydration and a skin protectant.
then.clinical_impact: Dry, cracked periwound skin breaks down and enlarges the wound.

rules.title: Tunneling with depth of 2 cm or more indicates hidden dead space

then.rationale: Tunneling with depth of 2 cm or more indicates hidden dead space. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Measure the tunnel direction and depth.
then.clinical_impact: Unmeasured dead space conceals infection and impairs packing.

rules.title: Undermining with a rolled or epibole edge indicates stalled edge advancement

then.rationale: Undermining with a rolled or epibole edge indicates stalled edge advancement. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Assess the wound edge for stalled advancement.
then.clinical_impact: Epibole prevents epithelial migration and stalls closure.

rules.title: Tunneling with purulent exudate suggests abscess or deep infection

then.rationale: Tunneling with purulent exudate suggests abscess or deep infection. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Evaluate urgently for abscess or deep infection.
then.clinical_impact: Purulent tunneling can indicate a deep collection needing drainage.

rules.title: Increasing undermining area with increasing wound size indicates wound deterioration

then.rationale: Increasing undermining area with increasing wound size indicates wound deterioration. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Reassess the wound etiology and treatment plan.
then.clinical_impact: Simultaneous expansion of undermining and size signals a failing plan.

rules.title: Wound size increased by at least 20 percent within two weeks indicates deterioration

then.rationale: Wound size increased by at least 20 percent within two weeks indicates deterioration. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Reassess the diagnosis and care plan for a deteriorating wound.
then.clinical_impact: Rapid enlargement indicates the current plan is failing.

rules.title: New or increasing pain with worsening appearance signals complication risk

then.rationale: New or increasing pain with worsening appearance signals complication risk. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Escalate to a clinician for a suspected complication.
then.clinical_impact: Rising pain with worsening appearance often precedes infection or ischemia.

rules.title: Fever or chills with wound infection signs indicates systemic infection risk

then.rationale: Fever or chills with wound infection signs indicates systemic infection risk. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Arrange urgent medical evaluation for systemic infection.
then.clinical_impact: Systemic signs with a wound source may indicate sepsis.

rules.title: Any wound in an immunocompromised patient with mild infection signs is a high-risk presentation

then.rationale: Any wound in an immunocompromised patient with mild infection signs is a high-risk presentation. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Lower the threshold to escalate given immunocompromise.
then.clinical_impact: Immunocompromised patients decompensate quickly from minor infection.

rules.title: Plantar DFU with absent or diminished pulses, pain, and reduced sensation suggests peripheral arterial disease

then.rationale: Plantar DFU with absent or diminished pulses, pain, and reduced sensation suggests peripheral arterial disease. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Order an ABI test and refer to vascular surgery.
then.clinical_impact: PAD in a diabetic foot markedly raises amputation risk.

rules.title: Venous ulcer at the medial malleolus present 12+ weeks with edema and stalled healing indicates chronicity

then.rationale: Venous ulcer at the medial malleolus present 12+ weeks with edema and stalled healing indicates chronicity. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Review compression and consider biopsy to rule out malignancy.
then.clinical_impact: A non-healing chronic venous ulcer can mask malignancy.

rules.title: Surgical incision within 7 days post-op with increasing drainage, separating edges, and obesity or diabetes risks dehiscence

then.rationale: Surgical incision within 7 days post-op with increasing drainage, separating edges, and obesity or diabetes risks dehiscence. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Obtain a surgical consult and provide wound support.
then.clinical_impact: Early post-op dehiscence risk warrants surgical review.

rules.title: Stage 2 pressure injury increasing in size across consecutive assessments with macerated periwound indicates deterioration

then.rationale: Stage 2 pressure injury increasing in size across consecutive assessments with macerated periwound indicates deterioration. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Reassess the support surface and increase repositioning.
then.clinical_impact: A stage 2 injury enlarging over multiple assessments is deteriorating.

rules.title: Purulent wound with spreading erythema over 2 cm, fever, and elevated WBC suggests systemic infection or sepsis

then.rationale: Purulent wound with spreading erythema over 2 cm, fever, and elevated WBC suggests systemic infection or sepsis. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Obtain blood cultures, start broad-spectrum antibiotics, and notify the physician urgently.
then.clinical_impact: This presentation may indicate sepsis and is immediately life-threatening.

rules.title: Full-thickness DFU with positive probe-to-bone present 6+ weeks suggests osteomyelitis

then.rationale: Full-thickness DFU with positive probe-to-bone present 6+ weeks suggests osteomyelitis. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Order X-ray/MRI and consider bone biopsy for osteomyelitis.
then.clinical_impact: Chronic full-thickness DFU with probe-to-bone strongly suggests osteomyelitis.

rules.title: Arterial ulcer on distal toes that is black or necrotic with severe pain worse on elevation suggests critical limb ischemia

then.rationale: Arterial ulcer on distal toes that is black or necrotic with severe pain worse on elevation suggests critical limb ischemia. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Obtain an emergent vascular consult and avoid debridement until perfusion is assessed.
then.clinical_impact: Critical limb ischemia is a limb-threatening emergency.

rules.title: ISTAP Type 1 skin tear with controlled bleeding and viable tissue is low risk

then.rationale: ISTAP Type 1 skin tear with controlled bleeding and viable tissue is low risk. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Approximate the skin flap and apply a non-adherent dressing.
then.clinical_impact: Low-risk skin tears heal well with flap approximation and atraumatic dressing.

rules.title: Chronic wound present 4+ weeks stalled despite standard care with persistent slough suggests biofilm

then.rationale: Chronic wound present 4+ weeks stalled despite standard care with persistent slough suggests biofilm. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Perform sharp debridement and trial an antimicrobial dressing.
then.clinical_impact: Suspected biofilm perpetuates chronic inflammation and stalls healing.

rules.title: Pressure injury with a wound bed obscured by eschar or slough and unknown depth is unstageable

then.rationale: Pressure injury with a wound bed obscured by eschar or slough and unknown depth is unstageable. Analytics identify this care gap from grounded evidence; a clinician decides. No claim change is proposed and human review is required.
then.recommended_action: Debride (if perfusion is adequate) to reveal the wound base and stage.
then.clinical_impact: An unstageable injury cannot be properly treated until the base is visible.
